text.title: spatial-ui-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the spatial-ui-patterns methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every UI panel MUST be assigned exactly one type ∈ {world-locked, head-locked, body-locked, hand-attached}. Multi-type panels are forbidden.

rationale.source: Apple visionOS HIG; Meta Quest UI guidelines

rule.rationale: Multi-type panels create conflicting expectations and break recenter affordances.

rule.statement: Head-locked UI is forbidden in visionOS production builds. On Quest / HoloLens / WebXR head-locked panels MAY exist only for safety telemetry (battery, hand-tracking-lost) with documented justification.

rationale.source: Apple visionOS HIG — head-locked content guidance

rule.rationale: Head-locked panels cause vestibular discomfort and break immersion; store reviewers reject them.

rule.statement: Body-locked and world-locked primary panels MUST be placed ≥1.0 m from the user. Default 1.5-2.0 m for sustained reading.

rationale.source: Meta Quest comfort docs; Apple visionOS HIG

rule.rationale: Closer than 1 m triggers vergence-accommodation conflict and eye strain within minutes.

rule.statement: Primary UI panel set MUST NOT exceed 40% of the user's horizontal field of view at default distance.
rule.rationale: Larger FoV occupancy causes peripheral-vision overload and reduces situational awareness.

rule.statement: Tappable controls MUST be ≥60×60 pt at 1.0 m distance. Smaller targets MUST scale with distance to maintain 60 pt visual angle.

rationale.source: Apple visionOS HIG — minimum target sizes

rule.rationale: Hand-ray jitter + eye-tracking accuracy at 1 m makes smaller targets miss-prone.
